raw_consumer_statement:
There was a charge for XXXX XXXX on my card. I tried to dispute it on line but was told to call the XXXX. I was transferred between4 different departments, made three seperate calls, asked to verify myself on each occasion and when they told me they were replacing my card for the second time, I expressed my disappointment for doing so without permission especially, since they did the same thing last month. I asked them to log a dispute but then they said they could not verify me and I was to call them from my office. I will cancel my card after making a final payment and trying once again to get the disputed charge remove, but am writing confirm everything I found on line aobut Citcard to be true : Many people consider the Citi card dispute process to be " awful '' because it can be frustratingly difficult to resolve disputes successfully, with complaints often citing long wait times, unhelpful customer service, and a tendency for Citi to deny legitimate claims, even in cases of fraud or clear errors ; essentially, many find the process of disputing a charge on a Citi card to be cumbersome and frustrating.

Key points about Citi card disputes : High denial rate : Some users report that Citi frequently denies disputes, even when presented with strong evidence, leading to frustration and a perception of unfair practices.

Complex process : Navigating the dispute process can be complicated, often requiring multiple calls and documentation, which can be time-consuming for cardholders.

Customer service issues : Users often complain about long wait times and unhelpful customer service representatives when attempting to resolve a dispute.

What to do if you have a Citi card dispute : Gather evidence : Be sure to have all relevant documentation, including receipts, statements, and communication with the merchant, before contacting Citi.

Contact customer service promptly : File a dispute as soon as you notice an unauthorized charge.

Be persistent : If your initial dispute is denied, be prepared to escalate the issue by contacting a supervisor or reaching out to the Consumer Financial Protection Burea
